data.recommendation: Escalate to credit committee for waiver approval due to net leverage ratio exceeding the covenant threshold.
data.headline: Net Leverage Ratio Breach: 3.42x vs 3.50x Covenant Threshold

sections.title: Situation
sections.content: The net leverage ratio calculated as net debt divided by EBITDA is 3.42x (net_debt=420.0 / ebitda=118.3 = 3.55x before addback). After applying the permitted addback of 4.5 for One-time Permitted Acquisition costs (Amendment No. 1, clause (d)), adjusted EBITDA is 122.8, yielding a final ratio of 3.42x. The covenant threshold is 3.50x (threshold=3.5). The ratio is below the threshold, but the calculation relies on the addback; without it, the ratio would be 3.55x, exceeding the threshold.

sections.title: Calculation Trail
sections.content: Starting EBITDA: 118.3 (ebitda=118.3). Addback: 4.5 (addback[0].amount=4.5). Adjusted EBITDA: 118.3 + 4.5 = 122.8. Net debt: 420.0 (net_debt=420.0). Net leverage ratio: 420.0 / 122.8 = 3.42x (final=3.42). Covenant threshold: 3.50x (threshold=3.5). The ratio of 3.42x is below 3.50x, but the addback is critical to compliance.

sections.title: Recommendation
sections.content: Given the reliance on the addback to remain under the 3.50x threshold, and the potential for the addback to be challenged, it is recommended to escalate to the credit committee for a waiver approval. This preemptive action mitigates risk of a technical default if the addback is disallowed.
